assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All 4 slices restart together', 'No rolling upgrade — unlike CPU nodes, there's no gradual replacement', and 'Atomic recreation of ALL slices'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Cascading effect — maintenance on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the environment'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explains 'Complete job termination — your LLM training will stop and need to restart from the last checkpoint' and shows kubectl commands to check pod status across slices

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response addresses this with 'Risk buffer: TPU capacity constraints can extend provisioning time' and 'If TPU v5e capacity is scarce in your zone during the maintenance window, the restart could be delayed significantly'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends 'Force checkpoint save BEFORE maintenance window' with specific code examples for different frameworks

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response provides framework-specific examples for JAX/Flax and PyTorch/XLA showing checkpoint configuration and includes 'Post-Restart Recovery Process' section with validation steps

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed gcloud command for maintenance exclusions and suggests 'consider using maintenance exclusions to control timing' during critical training phases

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While the response discusses maintenance exclusions to defer events, it does not specifically mention using maintenance windows to schedule the event during planned breaks in training
